title: Aum Shinrikyo
wikitext:
[]: Aum Shinrikyo Post by Evangelionfreak » Tue Jul 18, 2006 5:27 pm While cleaning my room yeaterday I found an issue of Anime Insider from January 2004, i found a giant Evangelion spread. So I said to myself ok I'll read it I thought it was very strange I would never ever do this because of evangelion! here read for yourself!! Sorry for the size http://i61.photobucket.com/albums/h60/Reikai_Tantei/annocult.jpg

[]: Post by System Shocker » Tue Jul 18, 2006 5:32 pm The Aum Shinrikyo were buddhists. And the Russian title for Neon Genesis Evangelion is "Евангелион" which is pronounced "Yevangelion" because they wouldn't have any words in Russian starting with an "A" sound, so they had to make it sound like "Yay" (my first language is Russian). So I don't know why it would be 'Evangelion' in Russian Aum broadcasts. Cool find though

[]: Post by Evangelionfreak » Tue Jul 18, 2006 5:33 pm System Shocker wrote: The Aum Shinrikyo were buddhists.

[]: Post by System Shocker » Tue Jul 18, 2006 5:39 pm Aum Shinrikyo, also known as Aum Supreme Truth and later as Aleph, was a Japanese religious group that mixed Buddhist and Hindu beliefs. The group gained international notoriety in 1995, when several of its followers carried out a poison gas attack on the Tokyo subways.

[]: Post by Evangelionfreak » Tue Jul 18, 2006 5:47 pm yeah i just thought that people trying to cause a third impact was bizare.

[]: Post by System Shocker » Tue Jul 18, 2006 5:52 pm Also, Pokemon Red, Blue, and Green came out in 1995, the same year Neon Genesis Evangelion did. I also find Anno's search for himself in his early life a bit interesting too, he actually could have been in that cult at some point.

[]: Post by Kaysow » Tue Jul 18, 2006 6:08 pm Smells pretty goddamned farfetched to me. That's one of the few news items I remember from my childhood. I was sitting in the car, opening packs of Turtlesstickers at some gas station when I heard on the radio how Japanese people were dying in the subways. Recently read up on it at this excellent site: http://www.crimelibrary.com/

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jul 18, 2006 7:44 pm Locked. Because I'm tired of hearing Matt Greenfield purporting this moronic theory that Anno and Evangelion are even remotely connected to this. The same sort of idea has been in dozens of other stories over the past several decades, and Evangelion exhibits no profound similarities to the cult in question.

[]: Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 19, 2006 12:22 am Dunno if you've ever read this one , Aaron. It seems different from the usual fare.
